overview: Jeskai Affinity is a fast, artifact-based aggressive deck that leverages All That Glitters to enchant creatures and deal accumulating combat damage. It merges the core Glitters game plan with Boros Synthesizer and third-color interaction like Galvanic Blast and Experimental Synthesizer for card advantage. While more flexible and resilient to specific hate than Azorius Affinity, it requires careful mana management and can be vulnerable to early graveyard hate like Dust to Dust.

keep_criteria: Hands with early artifacts like Frogmite or Novice Inspector
Mana base with access to red and white for Galvanic Blast and Glitters
At least one All That Glitters or Gingerbrute
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one mana accelerator (Thoughtcast, Talisman, or similar), and a turn-1 or turn-2 artifact or threat.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact synergy or early plays
Hands with only lands or high-cost cards like Gearseeker Serpent without protection
Hands vulnerable to early Dust to Dust without answers
Mulligan on pure land hands, multiple accelerators with no artifacts, or hands lacking a way to play a creature by turn 2.

examples.reason: Provides early artifact drops, mana flexibility, and a clear path to enchant a creature by turn 3.

early_game: Play artifact creatures like Frogmite and Novice Inspector to build affinity and board presence.
Use Springleaf Drum to fix mana and enable early colored spells.
Sequence Frogmites to cast Myr Enforcer on turn 2 and set up All That Glitters by turn 3.
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtcast or a cheap artifact like Glint Hawk or Ornithopter.
Turn 2: Play another land, cast a second accelerator or a medium threat (Memnite, Myr Retriever), or attack with evasive artifacts.
Sequence lands to ensure you can cast spells on curve while maintaining mana for instant-speed interaction.
mid_game: Deploy Gingerbrute or Glint Hawk to carry All That Glitters and apply immediate pressure.
Use Galvanic Blast post-combat to remove blockers or finish off creatures.
Cast Thoughtcast to dig for key cards and maintain card advantage.
Preserve mana for instant-speed interaction while maintaining artifact development.
Use Thoughtcast to dig for answers or threats.
Avoid overcommitting artifacts if the opponent has sweepers or targeted removal in hand.
Commit threats when you have protection or can trade favorably.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers, counters, or removal.
Use evasive artifacts to pressure while developing a bigger threat, and use instants to clear blockers or remove key threats.
late_game: Utilize Experimental Synthesizer for continuous value and artifact recursion.
Deploy Gearseeker Serpent as a resilient clock to close out games.
Sideboard in Relic of Progenitus or Destroy Evil to handle specific meta threats.
A massive artifact creature (e.g., Arcbound Ravager with +1/+1 counters, Myr Battlesphere, or a hasted threat) combined with burn or combat tricks.
Use Galvanic Blast to remove blockers or finish the opponent at 3-4 life.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to combat, the opponent has no sweepers, or you can trade artifacts favorably.
Commit when you can close the game in 1-2 turns and have enough interaction to protect your final attack.

key_cards.name: All That Glitters
key_cards.notes: Main win condition that accumulates damage on enchanted creatures; forces opponents to block or remove it.

key_cards.name: Gearseeker Serpent
key_cards.notes: Four-turn clock that pressures opponents and survives combat well, though vulnerable to removal.

key_cards.name: Gingerbrute
key_cards.notes: Haste and unblockable by non-creatures make it ideal for carrying Glitters and closing games quickly.

key_cards.name: Glint Hawk
key_cards.notes: Flying attacker that synergizes with Experimental Synthesizer and can reuse land drops.

key_cards.name: Frogmite
key_cards.notes: Low-cost artifact creature used to sequence turns, build affinity, and enable early Myr Enforcer and Glitters.

key_cards.name: Springleaf Drum
key_cards.notes: Provides colored mana flexibility in a 16-land deck and enables explosive early turns.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Blast
key_cards.notes: Main removal spell with greater reach; often used post-combat to clear blockers or finish creatures.

key_cards.name: Thoughtcast
key_cards.notes: Primary card draw engine that replaces Treasure Cruise, keeping the deck consistent and rewarding.

key_cards.name: Experimental Synthesizer
key_cards.notes: Generates card advantage, increases affinity, and synergizes heavily with Glint Hawk.

key_cards.name: Myr Enforcer
key_cards.notes: Holds back small creatures and poses as a threat; mandatory four-of in Pauper Affinity.

sequencing_tips: Sequence multiple Frogmites on turn 2 to cast Myr Enforcer and enchant a creature with All That Glitters by turn 3.
Cast Thoughtcast after playing a sequence of Frogmites to maximize card advantage.
Use Galvanic Blast after combat to remove blockers or finish off creatures that survived attacks.
Protect early Gearseeker Serpent with Dispel if the opponent has instant-speed removal.
Common mistake: Overcommitting artifacts without leaving mana for instant-speed interaction.
Always keep a mana source open for Galvanic Blast or counters.
Don't forget to use Thoughtcast to dig for answers or threats.
Watch for sweepers and hold up mana accordingly.
Jeskai Affinity thrives on tempo and resource advantage, so prioritize efficient trades and timely aggression.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in End the Festivities to take away board advantage and Dispel or Hydroblast for removal.
Sideboard in Relic of Progenitus to delay threats and protect the graveyard.
Sideboard in End the Festivities to clear the board and Dispel for counterspells.
Sideboard in Destroy Evil to handle their creatures and avoid lethal damage.
Use sideboard staples to handle their enchantments and board presence.
Mirror match; rely on sideboard interaction and board control.

Use Thoughtcast to dig for protection or threats. Cast spells in response to counterspells when possible. Rely on artifacts that enter the battlefield with immediate impact or cast multiple spells in a row to force counters and find answers.
Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use Galvanic Blast and other removal to kill their ramp creatures before they stabilize. Do not let them reach their late-game power level.

Use Glint Hawk and other evasive artifacts to trade efficiently. Hold up Galvanic Blast for their key threats. Stabilize with removal and then close the game with a big threat before they overwhelm you.

Pressure with cheap artifacts and cantrips. Force them to use resources on interaction. Close out games quickly before they can set up their win condition, and use instant-speed spells to disrupt their combo or control pieces.

Win by out-accelerating and out-resolving. Use Thoughtcast to dig deeper and find answers. Trade artifacts favorably, disrupt their acceleration with counters or destruction, and prioritize board presence over card advantage.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Wrath of God, Day of Judgment), targeted removal, counterspells, and graveyard recursion.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Its proposal is a mix of the Glitters game plan with Boros Synthesizer , with the addition of cards like Experimental Synthesizer for card advantage and Galvanic Blast for greater reach and interaction.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: All That Glitters is our main win condition and powerful “free-win” button at different points in the game.
Gearseeker Serpent is a four-turn clock that holds even larger creatures like Tolarian Terror , as well as putting as establishing a huge pressure if the opponent only has small creatures in play.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: if sequenced well, a second turn with multiple copies of it helps to cast Myr Enforcer to, on the third turn, enchant some creature with All That Glitters and close lethal damage in two attacks.
Using it after combat is more common than it might seem, but it also works as an excellent answer to Myr Enforcer , Avenging Hunter and other creatures we have trouble getting past.
cast Thoughtcast after a sequence of Frogmite s.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Relic of Progenitus is our default answer to delaying Tolarian Terror , Cryptic Serpent , and Gurmag Angler
less vulnerable to some hate, such as Standard Bearer
more likely to lose matches due to a well-applied Dust to Dust in the first turns.
Dispel is needed against Snuff Out , Counterspell , Deglamer and other troublesome spells.
